diagrams.title: 1's and 2's Complement Formation Process
diagrams.description: This diagram illustrates the step-by-step process of converting a positive binary number to its 1's and 2's complement representations. It shows the original 4-bit number (0101 = 5), the 1's complement result (1010), and the 2's complement result (1011 = -5). This visualizes the complement formation algorithm essential for understanding signed number representation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how 1's and 2's complements are formed from a positive binary number, demonstrating the bit inversion and optional +1 operation

diagrams.title: 2's Complement Number Range (4-bit Example)
diagrams.description: This diagram shows the complete range of values representable with 4-bit 2's complement numbers (-8 to +7). It visualizes how negative numbers are positioned at the high end of the binary range, with -1 represented as 1111 and -8 as 1000. This helps students understand the asymmetric range and why the most significant bit indicates sign.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the range of values in 2's complement representation and explains why there is one more negative value than positive (asymmetric range)
